name: Single Responsibility Principle
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 10: Classes
problem: A class has more than one reason to change because it handles multiple distinct concerns, leading to unexpected side effects when one responsibility changes and making the class difficult to reuse in isolation.
steps: Identify each distinct reason the class might need to change.
Extract each independent responsibility into its own small, focused class.
Ensure the resulting classes have cohesive methods that all relate to their single purpose.
Name each new class precisely after the responsibility it owns.
Apply SRP to methods too: a method that changes for more than one reason should be split.
